parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: I tried to shake this heavy feeling by going for a long walk and listening to my favorite playlist, but nothing actually worked. The quiet just kept echoing everything I have been avoiding lately. I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch, but the knot in my stomach refuses to loosen. It feels like I am constantly waiting for a shift that never really comes. I just want to feel light again without having to force it.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you're going through a really tough time, and it's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge these heavy feelings, and even more to try and actively shift them, like you did with your walk and music. It's frustrating when those things that usually help don't seem to make a difference, and the quiet just amplifies what you're trying to avoid.

That knot in your stomach is a really physical manifestation of what you're experiencing, and it's completely valid that it's not just loosening on command. It's like your body is holding onto that tension. And that feeling of waiting for a shift that never seems to arrive can be so draining.

It's a beautiful desire to feel light again, and the fact that you want it to happen naturally, without forcing it, speaks volumes about your self-awareness. Sometimes, when we're in the thick of it, the "rough patch" feels like it's going to last forever, even when we know logically it might not.

Please be gentle with yourself. It's okay that you're not feeling light right now. It's okay that the things you've tried haven't worked. Sometimes, the most helpful thing we can do is just acknowledge the weight, without judgment, and allow ourselves to feel it.

If you feel up to it, would you want to talk more about what you've been avoiding, or what you think might be contributing to this feeling? Sometimes just putting words to it can help, even if it doesn't immediately solve anything. Or, if you'd prefer, we can just sit with this for a bit. I'm here to listen.

turns.user_message: That same heavy feeling keeps creeping back every single week, no matter how many distractions I throw at it. I keep trying to reset my mindset by organizing my space or watching something funny, but the underlying tension always finds its way back. It is exhausting to pretend everything is fine when the silence between us just keeps stretching out. I feel like I am running in place while the distance quietly grows wider. I am starting to wonder if I will ever just feel at ease again.
turns.model_response:
It sounds incredibly draining to be caught in that cycle, feeling that heavy weight return week after week despite your best efforts to shake it off. It's completely understandable that you're feeling exhausted when you're putting so much energy into trying to reset and distract yourself, only to have that underlying tension resurface.

That feeling of pretending everything is okay while the silence stretches out must be so isolating. It's like you're putting on a brave face for the world, or perhaps for yourself, but it's taking a toll. And the metaphor of running in place while the distance grows wider really captures that sense of frustration and helplessness. It's hard to see progress when you feel like you're stuck.

It's also very natural to start questioning if you'll ever feel at ease again when you're in the thick of it like this. That's a significant worry to carry.

Thank you for sharing this with me. It takes a lot of courage to voice these feelings, especially when they're so persistent and difficult.

turns.user_message: I am completely drained from trying to manufacture some kind of normalcy out of this endless cycle of disappointment. Every little attempt to fix the mood just ends up highlighting how broken the rhythm between us really is. I keep replaying the last few months in my head, realizing how much of myself I have quietly folded away just to keep the peace. The weight of it all is suffocating, and I do not know how much longer I can pretend I am okay with this slow fade. I just feel so incredibly tired of carrying it alone.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you're going through an incredibly difficult and exhausting time. Feeling drained from trying to maintain a sense of normalcy when things feel so broken is completely understandable. It takes a tremendous amount of energy to try and mend a rhythm that feels off, and it's even harder when those attempts seem to just emphasize the disconnect.

It's also very brave of you to acknowledge how much of yourself you've been folding away to keep the peace. That kind of self-sacrifice, even with the best intentions, can lead to feeling suffocated and deeply tired. The realization that you've been carrying this burden alone must be incredibly heavy.

Please know that your feelings are valid. It's okay to feel this way, and it's completely understandable that you're tired of pretending. You don't have to carry this alone.
